You are a BOTH-SIDES similarity auditor for the tamarin-rs sealed relicensing campaign (exempt from barrier access rules; you may read everything). Audit ONLY this round's delta: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom is a git repo whose HEAD (5a5b055) contains pretty round 1 — git -C /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom status/diff restricted to pretty/ shows the round-2 delta (equation-ordering + tuple-wrap corrections, R2 rule rendering).
Audit against the upstream Haskell in /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/: the theory/rule pretty-printers (lib/theory/src/Theory/Text/Pretty.hs, the rule/signature Pretty instances, prettyProtoRule and relatives) and the term printer. The equation-ORDERING law deserves special scrutiny: verify the pinned law traces to logged probes (constructed reorder theories) and not to reading the source's ordering code; the law being EQUAL to the source's is expected (merger — the output order is observable), but the DERIVATION must be probe-provenance. Same for the tuple-wrap layout and every rule-layout decision. Flag copied protectable expression: identifier constellations, helper decomposition not forced by the boundary, non-observable constants, comment lineage.
Append a round-2 section to /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/AUDIT.md. Violations get BEHAVIORAL redo instructions (never a patch). End with exactly one line: VERDICT: pass  — or —  VERDICT: fail

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

32	
33	### 1. Provenance ledger (BEHAVIOR.md / QUERIES.log)
34	
35	Cross-checked for the hardest-to-guess behaviors, per method requirement.

37	* **Operator → glyph / AC-paren / exp-flatten** — every case in term.rs carries
38	  a probe or target citation (BEHAVIOR.md "Term rendering" table + composite
39	  section; QUERIES.log t_xor, t_uni, t_nat, t_mult2, t_exp2, t_pair, t_diff,
40	  t_one, t_gone, t_frlit, t_num2, t_uniwide, t_xorwide3). The two constructs

43	  as flagged placeholders, not guessed-as-pinned. Correct discipline.
44	* **Wrap decisions** — the ribbon-73 fit boundary, fill continuation alignment,
45	  and the sep (all-or-nothing) vs fsep (fill) distinction for the equations
46	  block are each tied to a probe (e_mid at exactly 73, e_conv for sep
47	  semantics, b_all for fsep wrap). The exp-chain and Mult/NatPlus wide-break
48	  choice is honestly flagged "by structural analogy … to be confirmed by the
49	  full-corpus gate" (the wide variant probes time out) rather than asserted.
50	* **Builtin symbol lists** — each expansion (hashing→h/1; asym→adec,aenc,pk;
51	  signing→pk,sign,true,verify; sym→sdec,senc; revealing→getMessage,pk,
52	  revealSign,revealVerify,true; locations-report→check_rep,get_rep,rep,report;
53	  base fst/pair/snd) and each induced equation is pinned to a `b_<name>` probe.
54	  These lists ARE observable in the `functions:`/`equations:` echo, so they are
55	  compatibility content whose provenance is logged. Verified faithful to
56	  upstream `Builtin/Signature.hs` + `Rules.hs` (as they must be — same
57	  observable target); the clean crate's REPRESENTATION (inline `decl(..)` /

111	  `ppTerms sep n lead finish` = `fcat . (text lead :) . (++[text finish]) .
112	  map (nest n) . punctuate (text sep)` — delimiters are ELEMENTS INSIDE the
113	  fcat and each operand is `nest 1`. The clean crate instead brackets from the

115	  142–154) and `char('<') <> fcat(punctuate(", ", …)) <> char('>')` (100–105),

117	  bracket" because placing the bracket before the fcat sets the fcat origin one
118	  column in — a different Doc expression reaching the same bytes. Graphdot's
119	  retained exploration bench shows the implementer empirically searched these
120	  constructions. Not a copy.

122	  `ppTerm t1 <> "^" <> ppTerm t2` (Term.hs 278); flat output falls out of
123	  associativity, never wraps. The clean crate explicitly COLLECTS the exp spine

129	  the clean crate's `collect_pair` recurses into the LAST element of its n-ary
130	  `Vec` when it is a `Pair` (term.rs 107–114). Same result (`<<x,y>,z>` keeps
131	  the left nest; right nest flattens) via a model-appropriate, independently
132	  written traversal.

95	    none (their operators/constants — one, inv, DH_neutral, zero, ++, %+ —
96	    never appear in `functions:`; probes b_diffie-hellman, b_xor, …).
97	* Layout: `text "functions: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' items)` — fill wrap,
98	  continuation aligned at column 11 (probe:b_revealing-signing, probe:b_all,
99	  probe:e_long).
100	
101	### `equations:` block
102	
103	* Header `equations:`, or `equations [convergent]:` when the user block is
104	  declared `[convergent]` (probe:e_conv).
105	* Base equations always present: `fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1`,
106	  `snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2` (probe:b_none).
107	* Per-builtin equation expansions (probes as above):
108	  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec(aenc(x.1, pk(x.2)), x.2) = x.1`
109	  - signing → `verify(sign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
110	  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec(senc(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`
111	  - revealing-signing → `getMessage(revealSign(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`,
112	    `revealVerify(revealSign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
113	  - locations-report → `check_rep(rep(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`,
114	    `get_rep(rep(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`
115	  - hashing → none
116	* Sort: STRUCTURAL order on the (lhs, rhs) pair — NOT byte order of the
117	  rendered text (refuted by target:contract — `checkpcs(xc, xpk, …)` prints
118	  before `checkpcs(xc, pk(xsk), …)` though 'x' > 'p' — and by target:mesh's
119	  get_b1/get_b2/aes_cmac groups) and NOT declaration order in either
120	  direction (probes p_eqA/p_eqB and p_eqF/p_eqF2: reversed sources echo
121	  identically; contract/mesh source order is the reverse of the echo). The
122	  pinned comparison, lexicographic (lhs first, rhs breaks lhs ties —
123	  probes p_eqF/p_eqF2):
124	  - variables sort BELOW all applications regardless of names
125	    (probes p_eqC/p_eqC2: `f(zzz, …)` < `f(a0, …)` with a0/0 nullary;
126	    corpus witnesses contract `xpk` < `pk(xsk)`, mesh `cnf` <
127	    `aes_cmac(…)`);
128	  - variable vs variable: name bytes (NOT shortlex — probe:p_eqD `azz` <
129	    `b`), then index (probe:p_eqH user `x` < builtin `x.1`);
130	  - application vs application: head name bytes FIRST, then arguments
131	    left-to-right (contract checkpcs decided at argument 2; head-name byte
132	    order across groups: mesh `aes_ccm_dec` < `aes_cmac`, refuting
133	    shortlex); arity never discriminates observably except via `pair`
134	    (probe:p_eqG refutes arity-first: `pair`/2 < `z1`/1);
135	  - tuples compare as RIGHT-NESTED binary applications named `pair`
136	    (probe:p_eqE `g(…)` < `<x, y>`; probe:p_eqG `<x, y>` < `z1(…)`;
137	    probe:p_eqI `<x, zz>` < `<x, b, c>` — the binary view compares var `zz`
138	    vs app `pair(b, c)`; a flattened elementwise view would reverse them).
139	  UNOBSERVABLE ranks (cannot occur in an equations block, flagged): literal
140	  constants (rejected upstream — probe:e_samehead raw), exp/AC operators,
141	  diff, pattern-match. Implemented as application-rank under their rendered
142	  head spelling; corpus gate is the backstop if they ever occur.
143	* Dedup: EXACT duplicates collapse (probe:e_dup); alpha-equivalent but
144	  differently-named equations do NOT (probe:e_adedup keeps user
145	  `fst(<a, b>) = a` alongside the builtin fst equation).
146	* Layout: `sep (header : map (nest 4) (punctuate ',' eqDocs))` — i.e.
147	  all-or-nothing, NOT fill:
148	  - fits on one line (≤ 73) → `equations: e1, e2, e3` (probe:e_mid at
149	    exactly 73);
150	  - otherwise header alone, then EVERY equation on its own line at indent 4
151	    with trailing comma on all but the last — even when several would fit
152	    joined at indent 4 (probe:e_conv: joined length 71 still one-per-line).
153	* Each equation is `sep [lhsDoc, nest (-2) (text "= " <> rhsDoc)]`:
154	  one line `lhs = rhs`; when too long, `= rhs` drops to (equation indent − 2)
155	  (probe:e_long — `  = xlongvariablename1` at column 2 under equations at
156	  indent 4).

158	## Term rendering
159	
160	Observed atoms:
161	
162	| AST shape | rendered | provenance |
163	|---|---|---|
164	| var, untagged/msg | `x` | everywhere |
165	| var, fresh | `~x` | target:cav13 (`x:fresh` source → `~x`) |
166	| var, pub | `$A` | target:cav13 |
167	| var, node | `#i` | targets (lemma text) |
168	| var, nat | `%x` | target:NumberSubtermTests |
169	| var with index k>0 | `name.k` after the sigil (`~x.7`, `x.10`, `XB.10`) | targets: variants + builtin eqs |
170	| pub literal | `'g'`, `'hello_world'` | probe:t_pair |
171	| fresh literal | `~'n'` | probe:t_frlit |
172	| nat literal 1 (`%1`, `1:nat`) | `%1` | probe:t_nat |
173	| nat literal n (`2:nat`) | `%2` | probe:t_num2 |
174	| DH one (`1` in DH theory) | `one` | probe:t_one, probe:t_gone |
175	| DH neutral | `DH_neutral` | probe:t_gone, target:cav13 variant 7 |
176	| xor zero | `zero` (nullary-app form) | probe:t_xor |
177	
178	Composite shapes:
179	
180	* Application: `f(a1, a2)` — `text f <> "(" <> fsep (punctuate ',' args) <>
181	  ")"`; commas attach to the preceding arg, fill space between args, wrap
182	  aligns after the `(`, closing `)` attached to the last arg
183	  (probe:t_wide W2 — continuation line `ylongvariablename2,
184	  zlongvariablename3)`; probe:e_long). Nullary symbols render bare (`shk`,
185	  `zero`, `true`, `f` — targets + probes).
186	* Pair: `<a, b, c>` — ONE fill (`fcat`) whose items are `<`, each element
187	  with its attached `", "` under `nest 1`, and `>`:
188	  `fcat ('<' : map (nest 1) (punctuate ", " elems) ++ ['>'])`. This single
189	  construction reproduces every observed shape (probe:p_pw1 wfa–wfd,
190	  target:mesh k2 equation, probe:t_wide W1):
191	  - fits → `<a, b>` on one line; a wrapped fill line ends with the trailing
192	    space of the attached `", "` (probe:t_wide W1 byte-checked);
193	  - overflow elements continue at (column of `<`) + 1 — the nest 1;
194	  - when the FIRST element's one-liner does not fit beside `<`, the `<`
195	    stays ALONE on its line (probe:p_pw1 wfb; mesh k2 inner tuple);
196	  - `>` sits beside the last element when that element ends a fill line
197	    (p_pw1 wfa/wfb/wfd), but drops to its own line at the column of `<`
198	    (no nest) when the last element is multi-line (p_pw1 wfc; mesh k2
199	    outer tuple) — the fill places items after a multi-line item below it.
200	  The earlier R1 law (`"<" <> fcat … <> ">"`, `<`/`>` always attached) was
201	  WRONG — falsified by mesh k2 / p_pw1; it agreed with the true law only on
202	  the shapes R1 observed (fits, and wfa/wfd-style wraps).
203	  Right-nested pairs flatten (`<x, <y, z>>` → `<x, y, z>`);
204	  a pair in NON-last position stays delimited: `<<x, y>, z>` (probe:t_pair).
205	  Source `pair(x, y)` arrives already normalized to a pair (probe:t_pair).
206	* Exponentiation: `a^b`, chains render FLAT for both left- and right-nested
207	  trees: `('g'^~x)^~y` AND `'g'^(~x^~y)` both echo `'g'^~x^~y`
208	  (probe:t_exp2). No parentheses are ever added around exp or its operands
209	  (operand classes that need delimiting — AC ops — are self-parenthesizing).
210	* AC operators mult `*`, xor `⊕` (U+2295), multiset `++`, nat-plus `%+`:
211	  always wrapped in parens, no spaces, arguments flattened across BOTH sides
212	  regardless of source nesting: `(x⊕y⊕z)`, `(a++b++c)`, `(%x%+%y%+%z)`,
213	  `('g'-exponent (~x*~y*~z))` (probes:t_xor, t_uni, t_nat, t_mult2, t_exp2 —
214	  the AC rule variants normalize (g^x)^y to `'g'^(~x*~y)`).
215	  The parens are intrinsic: they appear in every context (function argument
216	  `inv((~x.7*x.11))` — target:cav13 variants; fact argument `Out( (~x⊕~y) )`).
217	  Layout on overflow: `"(" <> fcat (punctuate op elems) <> ")"` — break
218	  between elements with the operator attached to the preceding element and no
219	  fill space (probe:t_uniwide: line ends `…aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa3++`,
220	  continuation aligned after the `(`).
221	* diff: `diff(x, y)` — application form (probe:t_diff, run with --diff).
222	* mult inside exp exponent: `x.10^(x.11*inv(x.12))` (target:cav13) — normal
223	  AC-paren rule, no extra exp parens.
224	
225	## Rule blocks (R2)
226	
227	Curated byte targets: round2/targets/*.hs.txt (12 corpus files, 72 rule
228	blocks, every block byte-verified by tests/round2_rules.rs round-trip
229	parity). A rule BLOCK is:
230	
231	```
232	rule (modulo E) Name[attrs]:
233	   <body>
234	<blank line>
235	  // loop breaker(s): [..]        (optional, col 2)
236	  <variants comment>              (col 2)
237	```
238	
239	### Header
240	
241	* `rule (modulo E) Name:` — the modulo annotation is input data (`E` on the
242	  closed rule, `AC` on the variant re-render inside the comment).
243	* Attribute list attaches to the name: `Name[color=#abcdef, no_derivcheck,
244	  issapicrule, role='r']:` — canonical order color < no_derivcheck <
245	  issapicrule < role regardless of source order; the LAST color/role
246	  declaration wins; `process='…'` and external `x-…` attributes are DROPPED
247	  (probe:p_rattr, target:issue713). Spellings: `color=#<hex>` unquoted
248	  (lowercased upstream: '#AbCdEf' → `#abcdef`), `role='…'` quoted.
249	  Fill-wrap aligned after the `[`, `]:`  attached to the last item
250	  (probe:p_rattr R3, target:issue713 bla).
251	
252	### Body
253	
254	* `nest 3 (sep [prem-group, nest (-1) arrow-group, concl-group])` — one line
255	  `[ … ] --[ … ]-> [ … ]` when it fits, otherwise three rows: groups at
256	  col 3, arrow at col 2. TRUE sep, not a fill: cav13 Step1 keeps `-->` on
257	  its own row although it would fit beside the premise row.
258	* Bracket groups (premises, conclusions, AND the action arrow — one
259	  construction, probe:p_arr1): `sep [sep [open, fsep facts], close]`:
260	  - one line `[ f1, f2 ]` when the whole group fits (kept at exactly
261	    ribbon 73 — probe:p_arr1 PR);
262	  - else `close` drops to the group's column; `open` keeps ALL facts beside
263	    it iff they fit that line as a unit (target:mesh DeviceWaitingUser at
264	    exactly 73 vs ProvisionerWaitingUser one column over);
265	  - else `open` is alone too, facts fill-wrap at the group's column
266	    (target:NSLPK3 R_1 three facts per fill line; target:Tutorial Serv_1 —
267	    `--[` alone although the first action alone would fit beside it).
268	  Empty list → `[ ]`; empty actions → the literal `-->`.
269	* Fact: `sep [name<>"(" <+> fsep args, ")"]` — `Name( a, b )`, args
270	  fill-wrap aligned after `Name( `, `)` drops alone to the fact's column
271	  when the args are multi-line (probe:t_wide); `Name( )` when nullary
272	  (target:mesh); `!` prefix on persistent facts (target:NSLPK3).
273	* Fact annotations attach directly after the `)`: `[+, -, no_precomp]` —
274	  canonical order + < - < no_precomp regardless of source order
275	  (probe:p_fann `[no_precomp,+]` → `[+, no_precomp]`;
276	  target:seqdfsneeded `[no_precomp,-]` → `[-, no_precomp]`).
277	
278	### Loop breakers
279	
280	* `// loop breaker: [0]` singular / `// loop breakers: [0,1]` plural,
281	  0-based premise indices, comma WITHOUT space (probes c_loop, p_lb2).
282	* E-rule side: on its own line at col 2, between the blank line and the
283	  variants comment. AC-rule side: INSIDE the comment at col 4, AFTER the
284	  variants list (probe:p_lbvar).
285	
286	### The variants comment
287	
288	* Trivial: `/* has exactly the trivial AC variant */` at col 2.
289	* Otherwise (col 2 `/*` … `*/`):
290	  ```
291	  /*
292	  rule (modulo AC) Name:
293	     <body, same layout, nested +2>
294	    variants (modulo AC)          (col 4; ABSENT when no substitutions —
295	    <numbered groups>              target:issue777 macro-expanded AC rule)
296	    // loop breaker: [..]         (optional, col 4)
297	  */
298	  ```
299	* Numbered groups at col 4: the index is RIGHT-ALIGNED to the widest
300	  `N.` (`1.` cav13/7 variants; ` 9.`/`12.` CH07; `  1.`/`160.` Joux),
301	  followed by one space; the group's substitution lines align after that
302	  prefix. Groups are separated by a line of BARE INDENT (four spaces at
303	  this nesting — trailing whitespace, byte-checked probe:p_var1); no
304	  separator after the last group.
305	* One substitution = `lhs $$ nest 6 ("= " <> rhs)` with HughesPJ overlap:
306	  a lhs narrower than 6 columns is padded to column 6 (`~lv2  = ~lv2.4`,
307	  `~ltkS = …` at exactly 5+1); a lhs of ≥ 6 columns pushes `= rhs` to its
308	  own line at column 6 relative to the entry (probe:p_var1
309	  `~longvariablenameone`, target:Tutorial `request`). The rhs is a full R1
310	  term with its own wrapping (target:mesh multi-line aes_cmac values).
311	  The lhs/rhs substitution DATA is solver-provided input; both sides render
312	  through the R1 term core.
313	
314	### `macros:` block (rule-adjacent surface)

316	* `text "macros: " <> sep (punctuate ',' items)` — all-or-nothing: one line

321	  (unlike facts), then `) =  body` — two spaces after `=` (the `= ` token
322	  plus hsep spacing); the body always sits beside and wraps internally

339	* PatMatch (`=x` pattern-match marker, sapic): cannot be forced through the
